revoked."},"version()":{"notice":"Returns the full semver contract version."}},"notice":"EAS schema resolver for the ZK-Wordle \"play with friends\" record \u2014 the attestation twin of WordleLog.logSolve: an attestation exists only if the wordle_solve proof verifies against the challenge's committed word and the committed dictionary. See SudokuSolveResolver for the design rationale (proof-gated attestations, EAS-native indexing, parallel to the Log path). SCHEMA (field order is load-bearing \u2014 it IS the attestation encoding; register with revocable=false):   uint256 challengeId,uint256 guessesUsed,uint256 guessesCommit,uint256[24] proof SOLVER IDENTITY: the wordle_solve circuit carries no player signal (WordleLog binds msg.sender), so here the solver is the attestation's `recipient`, and it must equal the `attester` \u2014 the same \"whoever submits it, claims it\" trust level as WordleLog, made explicit. Solve time is EAS-native `attestation.time`; guesses-used is the ranked returns._0: Whether the resolver supports ETH transfers.

params.values: Explicit ETH amounts which were sent with each attestation.

returns._0: Whether all the attestations are valid.

params.attestations: The existing attestations to be revoked.
params.values: Explicit ETH amounts which were sent with each revocation.

returns._0: Whether the attestations can be revoked.

params.attestation: The existing attestation to be revoked.

returns._0: Whether the attestation can be revoked.
Semver contract version as a string.

attest((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,ui.notice: Processes an attestation and verifies whether it's valid.

attested(uint256,uint256).notice: One attestation per (challenge, guess sequence) — the same anti-replay key WordleLog uses, in this resolver's own book so the two canonical records stay independent.

isPayable().notice: Checks if the resolver can be sent ETH.

multiAttest((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint.notice: Processes multiple attestations and verifies whether they are valid.

multiRevoke((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint.notice: Processes revocation of multiple attestation and verifies they can be revoked.

revoke((bytes32,bytes32,uint64,uint64,ui.notice: Processes an attestation revocation and verifies if it can be revoked.

version().notice: Returns the full semver contract version.
